meta.release_notes:
# Changelog
All notable changes to this model will be documented in this file.

## [Unreleased]

## [3.0.0] - 2025-07-07
A conversion table from previous version 2.0.0 to 3.0.0 of this model is provided with the updated KIT Quality Management for CX release Saturn.

### Added
- model feature additionalInformation key:value pair added

### Changed
- to reduce redundancies in modelling some properties are now used form share quality core model
- using parts definition from shared quality core model (via samm:extends) to unify ECU properties 
- at least one primary identifier per entity is now mandatory
- diagnosticSessions_anonymizedVIN, diagnosticSessions_sessionStart, diagnosticSessions_qualityTaskId are now mandatory properties

### Removed
- diagnosticSessions_ecuList_hwPartNumber
 redundant property to diagnosticSessions_ecus_partNumber




## [2.0.0] - 2024-01-29
### Added
- metaInformation property added to root entity
- integration of the shared UUID characteristic and RegEx for Catena-X identifiers
- introducing new characteristic for unique identifiers
- end date of a diagnostic session added
- procedures entity added to support failure-tree-based analysis in the workshop: Procedures can have sub-procedures could also be linked to ecuList
- for Ecus: calibration and variant coding properties

### Changed
- changed all properties to optional except unique identifier per entity
- Clear structure: session has ecuList as child, ecuList has dtcList as child, dtcList has envConditionList as child

### Removed
- vehicle entity: Properties now located one level up on DiagnosticSession
- eventList: Removed completely - instead procedures list was introduced

## [1.0.0] - 2022-12-13 -> deprecated
### Added
- initial version of this model

### Changed
n/a

### Removed

nodes.label: DiagnosticData
nodes.description: A Catena-X semantic data model for a list of vehicle diagnostic sessions.

nodes.label: diagnosticSessions
nodes.description: A list of diagnostic sessions.

nodes.label: metaInformation
nodes.description: This meta information is valid for all entries in this data set. The meta information is used to tell the data consumer which selection criteria were applied by the data provider.

nodes.label: recordStatus
nodes.description:
The record operation enumeration can be used to realize delta update concept.
Delta update concept means:
- You transfer an initial load of data
- After the first week only the delta to the initial load is transferred


The record status describes whether this record is:
- new=>This record is transferred the first time
- update=> Some properties of this record have changed compared to a previous transfer
- delete=> This record was transferred in the initial load or in a previous delta update, but is not used any more and therefore it should be deleted on data consumer side
- same=> This record was transferred in the initial load or in a previous delta update

nodes.label: sessionId
nodes.description: A oem-specific session identifier that is unique in this company.

nodes.label: anonymizedVIN
nodes.description: OEM-specific hashed VIN. Unique within OEM.

nodes.label: catenaXQualityTaskId
nodes.description: The fully anonymous and unique Catena-X ID of this quality task. This id is unique inside Catena-X data space.

nodes.label: catenaXVehicleId
nodes.description: A unique identifier for the vehicle in the Catena-X data space.

nodes.label: countryCode
nodes.description: Country code in  ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 codes where this session took place

nodes.label: diagnosticSoftwareName
nodes.description: The name of the diagnostic software that was used.

nodes.label: diagnosticSoftwareVersion
nodes.description: The version of the used diagnostic software

nodes.label: mileage
nodes.description: Mileage counter of the car.

nodes.label: sessionEnd
nodes.description: Describes the end point for this diagnostic session.

nodes.label: sessionStart
nodes.description: Describes the starting point for this diagnostic session.

nodes.label: vehicleSoftwareCategory
nodes.description: Software category of this car during the session - only available for OEMs that have a software category on vehicle level.

nodes.label: vehicleSoftwareVersion
nodes.description: Software version of this car during the session - only available for OEMs that have a software category on vehicle level.

nodes.label: workshop
nodes.description: Groups workshop-related properties

nodes.label: ecuList
nodes.description: List of ECUs that had an entry in its internal failure memory during the diagnostic session

nodes.label: procedures
nodes.description: List of procedures / sub-procedures.

nodes.label: selectionCriteria
nodes.description: Describes the selection criteria a Catena-X data provider has applied to create this data set.

nodes.label: selectionStart
nodes.description: Describes the starting point for providing records in this data set.

nodes.label: selectionEnd
nodes.description: Describes the end point for providing records in this data set.

nodes.label: workShopId
nodes.description: OEM-specific identifier for a workshop.

nodes.label: latitude
nodes.description: Latitude property for GPS coordinates.

nodes.label: longitude
nodes.description: Longitude property for GPS coordinates.

nodes.label: oemSerialNumber
nodes.description: Serial part number of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM). Typically the OEM is installing the part in the car. A serial part number is unique for every single part.

nodes.label: assemblyPartNumberVersion
nodes.description: OEM-specific ecu assembly version

nodes.label: catenaXPartId
nodes.description: The fully anonymous and unique Catena-X ID of this part. This id is unique inside Catena-X data space.

nodes.label: calibrationVersion
nodes.description: Calibration version that was put on this ecu. A calibration file works like a parameter file for the ecu software.

nodes.label: hwPartNumber
nodes.description: hardware part number of ECU

nodes.label: hwVersion
nodes.description: hardware version of ECU

nodes.label: oemPartName
nodes.description: Part name as defined by the original equipment manufacturer (OEM). Typically the OEM is installing the part in the car.

nodes.label: oemPartNumber
nodes.description: Part number of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM). Typically the OEM is installing the part in the car. A part number is NOT unique: All parts of the same kind share the same part number.

nodes.label: readOutDate
nodes.description: Date when this ECU information was read out from the diagnostic session

nodes.label: swPartNumber
nodes.description: SW part number of this ecu

nodes.label: swVersion
nodes.description: current version of the software on this ecu

nodes.label: variantCoding
nodes.description: Variant coding that was put on this ecu. Variant coding tells the ecus software how to behave.

nodes.label: dtcs
nodes.description: list of diagnostic trouble codes

nodes.label: procedureID
nodes.description: A unique identifier for this procedure/sub-procedure call

nodes.label: procedureDescription
nodes.description: An optional description for this procedure

nodes.label: procedureEnd
nodes.description: End date and time for this procedure call.

nodes.label: procedureStart
nodes.description: Start date and time for this procedure call.

nodes.label: procedureResult
nodes.description: Result of the procedure

nodes.label: subProcedures
nodes.description: A list of sub-procedures

nodes.label: dtcHexValue
nodes.description: Hex value of this DTC

nodes.label: faultPath
nodes.description: OEM-specific: Fault path for this DTC. Allows further analysis

nodes.label: faultPathDescription
nodes.description: OEM-specific description of DTC fault path

nodes.label: freezeFrame
nodes.description: freeze frame from ecu. The freeze frame records many parameters of the DTC and surrounding parameters like outside temperature when the DTC was set. It is a very long HEX string with many OEM-specific and ECU-specific content in

nodes.label: fullDescription
nodes.description: description of DTC and failure byte. Both description strings are concatenated using a "-" as separator

nodes.label: fullName
nodes.description: Combined string of DTC name  plus the so called DTC sub type or DTC failure byte. DTC name starts with B|C|P|U + 4 hex chars + separator "-" + DTC failure byte: 2 hex chars. As this only applies for standardized DTCs there is no enforcement.

nodes.label: isMilOn
nodes.description: describes whether this DTC set the MIL (malfunction indicator light)  in the dashboard

nodes.label: occurenceCounterTotal
nodes.description: Counter how often this DTC was set in total

nodes.label: occurenceMileage
nodes.description: mileage in km when the DTC occurred the first time

nodes.label: occurenceTimeStamp
nodes.description: date and time when the DTC occured the first time/was recorded the first time in the ECU

nodes.label: state
nodes.description: OEM-specific state of DTC: 0;1 (permanent/temporary/intermediate), could also be a string with permanent, temporary, intermediate, ....

nodes.label: type
nodes.description: Indicator whether this DTC was stored as Error or Info

nodes.label: envConditionList
nodes.description: A list of environment conditions: E.g. outside temperature measured by the vehicle, a specific value measured by on ECU, ....

nodes.label: conditionId
nodes.description: OEM-specific: Primary key for this condition consists of unique identifier of env. condition and DTC

nodes.label: conditionCreationTimeStamp
nodes.description: Date and time when this condition/information was created.

nodes.label: conditionDescription
nodes.description: The description of the environment condition/information

nodes.label: conditionValue
nodes.description: The numeric value of the condition as double.

nodes.label: measurementUnit
nodes.description: The unit of measurement for the environment condition value.

nodes.label: SessionList
nodes.description: A list of diagnostic sessions coming from multiple vehicles.

nodes.label: EcuList
nodes.description: A list of electronic control units in this diagnostic session.

nodes.label: ProcedureList
nodes.description: A list of procedure calls in the diagnostic software.

nodes.label: DTCList
nodes.description: A list of DTCs that are present in this diagnostic session.

nodes.label: SubProceduresList
nodes.description: A list of sub-procedures

nodes.label: EnvironmentConditions
nodes.description: A list of environment conditions, like surrounding temperature, rpm, ...

nodes.label: MetaCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic for the meta information.

nodes.label: UniqueID
nodes.description: This Characteristic describes a property that is truly unque either inside the Company that is providing the data or even in Catena-X dataspace. Only one property in a semantic entity should be a uniqueID. Unique ids can be used to link a data set with other semantic models.

nodes.label: WorkshopCharacteristic
nodes.description: Groups all workshop properties.

nodes.label: CountryCodeCharacteristic
nodes.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 – three-letter country codes

nodes.label: Float
nodes.description: Characteristic of a float value.

nodes.label: Long
nodes.description: Characteristic for a long value.

nodes.label: HexCodeCharacteristic
nodes.description: Describing a hex code: 0-9, a-f, A-F, optional 0x

nodes.label: DiagnosticSession
nodes.description: One diagnostic session of one vehicle: Either this is a diagnostic session done in a workshop or via over-the-air diagnostic.

nodes.label: MetaInformation
nodes.description: This entity groups all meta information properties. Meta information properties give hints for a Catena-X data consumer to better understand, how this data set was created on the Catena-X data provider side.

nodes.label: WorkshopProperties
nodes.description: All properties to clearly identify a workshop.

nodes.label: ECU
nodes.description: A single ECU that is present/has a DTC set in the diagnostic session

nodes.label: ProcedureCall
nodes.description: One procedure call in the diagnostic software.

nodes.label: DiagnosticTroubleCode
nodes.description: diagnostic trouble codes or short DTCs are used inside ECUs to monitor failures. They were introduced for measuring vehicle emissions. Major DTCs for emissions are standardized by ISO standard ISO 15031-6:2015 - so called OBD2 standard. Over time DTCs were also introduced in other ECUs also besides engine and emission control. Many DTCs are vehicle manufacturer specific.

nodes.label: EnvironmentCondition
nodes.description:
One environment condition like temperature, rpm,...
If the environment condition was measured on vehicle level -> ecuSerialPartNumber is empty

nodes.label: RecordStatusEnumeration
nodes.description: Defining the enumeration for the record status

nodes.label: ProcedureResultCharacterisitc
nodes.description: Characteristic for procedure results.

nodes.label: TypeEnumumeration
nodes.description: Enumeration for a DTC.

nodes.label: CountryCodeTrait
nodes.description: Trait to ensure standard data format for country code

nodes.label: LatitudeTrait
nodes.description: Trait to define the latitude.

nodes.label: LongitudeTrait
nodes.description: Trait to define the longitude.

nodes.label: HexValueTrait
nodes.description: This trait allows 0-9, a-f, A-F in a string value. Optional prefix is "0x".

nodes.label: MileageCounter
nodes.description: The mileage counter of the car.

nodes.label: CountryCodeRegularExpression
nodes.description: Regular Expression that ensures a three-letter code

nodes.label: HexRegularExpression
nodes.description:
This regular expression enforces a hex string. It ensures 0-9, a-f, A-F with an optional prefix 0x.
Valid examples:
- 0x0123456789abcdefABCDEF
- 0123456789abcdefABCDEF

nodes.label: Double
nodes.description: A double value
